methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Starfish Space is a Series B frontier-tech company in Space & Aerospace founded by Austin Link and Trevor Bennett, having raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, including same-stage benchmarks Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $100M raised proxy, implying a $190M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $400M implied valuation, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data for the target. The public Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 53.2x P/S, but without Starfish Space's revenue, this method is inapplicable.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a range of $162M–$460M, with a median of $295M. The substantial dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the peer set, particularly the $1B+ spread between Aalyria and Sift at the same stage. Key uncertainties include the target's actual revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether stage-progression assumptions reflect current market conditions in frontier aerospace, where venture pace varies significantly.
